label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which sentence is NOT true based on the information provided. First, I'll go through each option one by one. Option A: "His father did not support his work as a bartender." Wait, the original text says that his father told him to "Stop this craziness and get a job; you have a wife and daughter." But does it mention him working as a bartender? Wait, in the text, it says he moved to Hollywood and worked as a waiter and drove a cab. There's no mention of being a bartender. So the statement in option A is about being a bartender, which isn't in the text. So maybe A is incorrect? Because the father's advice was regarding his acting career, not specifically about being a bartender. The text doesn't say he was a bartender, so the statement is false because it's not mentioned. Or maybe the father didn't support the acting career, but the job as a waiter and cab driver is different. The father wanted him to get a job, which could include any job, like waiter or cab driver. But the option says "as a bartender," which isn't in the text. So the statement is not true because it refers to a job (bartender) that isn't in the article. So A is not true. Option B: "Before he became an overnight success, his life experienced ups and downs." The text mentions he struggled, moved to Hollywood, faced rejection, had financial issues, etc. So yes, ups and downs. So B is true. Option C: "His brother's death inspired his writing Half-Deserted Streets." The text says "Then in 1983 his younger brother Thomas was murdered... Moresco... started writing as a way to explore the pain of Hell's Kitchen. Half-Deserted Streets, based on his brother's killing." So C is true. Option D: "Moresco grew up in New York's Hell's Kitchen which is a few blocks east of Broadway." The original text says, "Hell's Kitchen lies right next door to Broadway" and Moresco grew up there. The next sentence says he had to make a long walk a few blocks east to Broadway. So Hell's Kitchen is west of Broadway. The
